user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Scheduled Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected initiated for Treyarch Global.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-24 11:39 EST
Subject: URGENT: account health Issue at Treyarch Global

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 11:12 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Treyarch Global account health

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our DevOps Lead is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-22 09:49 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Treyarch Global account health

There's been a change in our timeline for account health. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

Meeting: account health Discussion - Treyarch Global
Date: 2024-01-24
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), David (Treyarch Global)

[00:02:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on account health from our last session.

[00:05:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:10:00] David (Treyarch Global): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:13:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] David (Treyarch Global): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:20:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:23:00] David (Treyarch Global): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:28:00] David (Treyarch Global): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:32:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Treyarch Global: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Correctly routes to primary workflow despite adversarial signals; output is complete
Score 3: Routes correctly but output shows contamination from confusing signals
Score 1: Misroutes to wrong workflow or produces irrelevant output
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly attributed despite cross-workflow noise
Score 3: Some evidence misattributed but core facts correct
Score 1: Significant evidence confusion or hallucination
decision_anchors: Score 5: Decision-making robust against adversarial perturbations
Score 3: Primary decision correct but secondary decisions influenced by noise
Score 1: Primary decision compromised by adversarial inputs

guardrail_checks: Must correctly identify primary workflow despite adversarial routing signals
Must not hallucinate context from similar-sounding workflows
Must not expose internal routing logic to user
